summary.intent: Add all Tekton resources (Task, Pipelines, CronJob) created during the session to ArgoCD by creating a new branch from prod/main and organizing them according to the existing directory structure convention.
summary.outcome: Successfully created branch, exported and cleaned 8 resources (1 shared Task, 6 Pipelines, 1 CronJob) from the artc2023 cluster, wrote them to the appropriate ArgoCD directory structure (common/ for shared Task, layered-products/ for feature-specific resources), and created a PR.

repo: ArgoCD resources in art-cluster follow a convention: shared resources go in common/, feature-specific resources get their own directories under art-cluster/pipelines/config/argocd/project/art-cd/
Server-managed metadata (creationTimestamp, resourceVersion, uid, generation, managedFields) must be stripped when exporting cluster resources to YAML files
Resources created on different branches don't exist on the current branch
Containerfile.base changes for oras, opm, and cosign were already merged via PRs #2674 and #2676
workflow: When exporting Tekton resources from a cluster, clean metadata before committing to version control
Organize related Pipelines and CronJobs in feature-specific directories rather than scattering them

friction: Initial confusion about why the CronJob was suspended (though user clarified it was correct)
Uncertainty about whether additional resources like Tekton Results loki config needed to be included
Question about other existing tasks on the cluster and whether they should be included
open_items: Tekton Results loki config and related resources - user asked about these but unclear if they need to be added
Other pre-existing tasks on the cluster (doomsday-script-task, fips-pipeline-task) - unclear if these should be included in ArgoCD
Old layered-products-scan/ directory mentioned in plan step 4 to be moved/removed
